assumptions: The recognised dividend receivable is an intragroup monetary item in consolidated financial statements.
The intragroup entities involved may have different functional currencies, so foreign exchange differences may not be fully eliminated on consolidation.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements rather than separate or individual financial statements.

approaches.label: General hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes the IFRS 9 hedge accounting model for recognised items, forecast transactions and components, including rules on which intragroup items may be designated in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context separately includes the hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk on a net investment in a foreign operation, which is a distinct IFRS hedge model relevant to intragroup foreign currency exposures at the consolidated level.
